default_creds.context: PostgreSQL DB_USERNAME/DB_PASSWORD in docker/.env.example and docker-compose.yaml (CVE-2025-56157); not published to host by default in 1.0.1+ but reachable from any compromised container/sidecar.
REDIS_PASSWORD default in docker/.env.example line 61; also embedded in CELERY_BROKER_URL redis://:difyai123456@redis:6379/1.

install_tell: git clone https://github.com/langgenius/dify.git && cd dify/docker && cp .env.example .env && docker compose up -d  # then open http://localhost/install
misconfig_patterns: Operator runs cp .env.example .env without setting INIT_PASSWORD, then exposes port 80 to the internet before completing /install -> first-visitor claimable admin (GET /console/api/setup == not_started)
Leaving DB_PASSWORD/REDIS_PASSWORD at difyai123456 default -> trivial lateral movement from any container/SSRF foothold to the data + vector store
SECRET_KEY left empty in multi-replica deploys -> inconsistent session signing / token forgery risk across nodes
ALLOW_REGISTER / ENABLE_EMAIL_PASSWORD_LOGIN left open on an internet-facing instance -> arbitrary account self-registration into the workspace
plugin_daemon debug port 5003 exposed to host and reachable from outside the trust boundary
Running a version below the SSRF/auth-bypass fix line while exposing /console/api/workspaces/.../tool-provider/api/test/pre

active_probe.false_positive_check: Do NOT fingerprint on generic strings ("AI", "chat", "workflow") — they collide with thousands of unrelated SaaS hosts and trip the ~50% rule. The vendor-unique discriminator is the unauthenticated GET /console/api/setup returning a JSON body whose top-level key is exactly "step" with value "not_started" or "finished" (plus optional "setup_at" ISO timestamp). This route+schema pair is Dify-specific and not present on LangFlow/Flowise/n8n/RAGFlow. Confirm a second marker (GET /console/api/version returns {"version": ..., "release_date": ...} or /console/api/system-features returns a JSON object with sso_enforced_for_signin / enable_email_password_login keys) before labeling a host Dify. A bare 200 on /install with a Next.js shell alone is insufficient — many SPA builders serve _next. The setup-step JSON is the load-bearing confirmation. A {"step":"not_started"} read on an internet-exposed host is itself the finding: an un-bootstrapped instance whose admin is claimable.

deployment_tells: nginx reverse proxy on 80/443 fronting api:5001 and web:3000 (only nginx + plugin_daemon publish to host)
docker-ssrf_proxy-1 sidecar container (squid on 3128) is a Dify-specific compose tell
plugin_daemon debugging port 5003 published to host by default (EXPOSE_PLUGIN_DEBUGGING_PORT)
11-service compose stack: api, worker, worker_beat, web, db(postgres), redis, sandbox, ssrf_proxy, plugin_daemon, weaviate/qdrant, nginx
SECRET_KEY auto-generated into persistent storage if left empty; PLUGIN_DAEMON_KEY / PLUGIN_DIFY_INNER_API_KEY ship with sample values in .env.example
/install Next.js (_next) SPA on first run; main app served from / after setup
pivot_paths: GET /console/api/setup -> {"step":"not_started"} signals an un-bootstrapped instance whose admin is claimable by the next visitor (read-only detection of the claimable-admin window — do NOT POST)
GET /console/api/version -> exact build for CVE scoping (e.g. < 1.14.2 -> file-preview bypass; <= 1.5.1 -> default DB creds)

GET /console/api/system-features -> reveals sso_enforced_for_signin / enable_email_password_login / enable_social_oauth_login to map the login surface and whether self-registration (ALLOW_REGISTER) is open
Favicon hash + nginx Server header + presence of plugin_daemon:5003 confirm Dify vs sibling builders without auth
If a marketplace/app share link (/chat/<token> or /completion/<token>) is public, it reveals app naming and prompt framing (business-logic leakage) without console access
vulnerabilities: CVE-2025-56157 (CVSS 9.8) — Default hard-coded PostgreSQL creds postgres/difyai123456 in docker-compose.yaml; affects Dify <= 1.5.1 (vendor notes Postgres not host-exposed by default since 1.0.1).
CVE-2025-63386 — Auth bypass via CORS misconfiguration on /console/api/setup: reflects arbitrary Origin with Access-Control-Allow-Credentials:true; affects v1.9.1.
CVE-2026-41949 (Dify < 1.14.2) — File-preview authorization bypass: any authenticated user reads up to 3000 chars of any uploaded doc cross-tenant by file UUID.
CVE-2026-41947 (CVSS 9.1, Dify <= 1.14.1) — Trace-configuration authorization bypass: editor user enables trace config for any app across tenants.
CVE-2024-10252 (CVSS ~7.2, Dify <= 0.9.1) — Code injection via internal SSRF in sandbox service; authenticated high-priv RCE as root in sandbox.
CVE-2024-12775 — SSRF via Create-Custom-Tool test endpoint POST /console/api/workspaces/current/tool-provider/api/test/pre (Dify 0.10.1).
CVE-2024-11822 — SSRF (open issue tracked against current versions).
GHSA-6pw4-jqhv-3626 — Unauthorized access/modification of app orchestration.
Note: 2025-10/11 reports (issues #29277/#29278) describe active RCE exploitation (miner+backdoor) prompting a re-tag of v1.10.1-fix.1 image.
auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: docker/.env.example ships INIT_PASSWORD= empty (line 27) and SECRET_KEY= empty (line 26). The documented quickstart (docs.dify.ai/en/self-host/quick-start/docker-compose: copy .env.example to .env, docker compose up -d, then visit http://localhost/install) forces a setup wizard that creates the admin account before the app is usable. api/controllers/console/setup.py gates POST /console/api/setup with `if get_setup_status(): raise AlreadySetupError()` AND `if tenant_count > 0: raise AlreadySetupError()` AND `if not get_init_validate_status(): raise NotInitValidateError()`. After admin creation the UI and /console/api require a logged-in session (cookie/JWT). So a completed deployment is auth-on-default. CAVEAT (the load-bearing nuance): the INIT_PASSWORD gate is the ONLY barrier on first-run admin creation, and api/controllers/console/init_validate.py returns True (status "finished") when INIT_PASSWORD is unset — `if os.environ.get("INIT_PASSWORD"):` is skipped entirely and the function falls through to `return True`. Since the default .env.example leaves INIT_PASSWORD empty, a fresh instance exposed on port 80 BEFORE the operator completes /install is a claimable-admin race: the first anonymous visitor can POST /console/api/setup and become admin with no init-password challenge. Posture is therefore auth-on-default for an established install, but with a configurable-default-off first-run window driven by the empty INIT_PASSWORD default.
sensitive_assets: Upstream LLM provider API keys (OpenAI/Anthropic/Azure/Bedrock) configured per-workspace in model providers — readable/usable once admin claimed
Ingested RAG knowledge-base documents and any PII/PHI within them (file preview leaks up to 3000 chars cross-tenant per CVE-2026-41949)
Visual workflow / agent / chatflow definitions revealing internal business logic, prompts, and tool wiring
End-user conversation history and message logs per app
Tool/API credentials stored in custom tool providers (API keys, OAuth secrets)
PostgreSQL data store (default creds postgres/difyai123456 — CVE-2025-56157, not host-exposed by default since 1.0.1+) and Weaviate/Qdrant/pgvector vector store contents
Plugin daemon inner-API keys and SECRET_KEY material if .env left at shipped defaults
sources: [CANDIDATE - NuClide Cat-34 rag-app-builders Stage -1 OSINT 2026-06-12; software-default characterization, no live host probed yet]
